bodyHtml:
claude-codex-usage-dashboard is making waves in the Frontend ecosystem this week, gaining 18 new stars. With a total of 140 stars on GitHub and 23 forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 140Stars
📈 +18/wkGrowth
🔀 23Forks
⚠️ 1Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify

Why claude-codex-usage-dashboard Is Trending

The Frontend landscape is constantly evolving, and claude-codex-usage-dashboard has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 18 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: A local Windows dashboard for Claude Code and Codex usage limits.

If you're working in Frontend, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Practical Use Cases

Here are some common scenarios where claude-codex-usage-dashboard shines:

  1. New projects: Start your next JavaScript project with this tool as a foundation
  2. Migration: Consider switching if your current solution is slowing you down
  3. Learning: Study the source code to understand modern Frontend patterns

Final Thoughts

claude-codex-usage-dashboard is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
